## ARCHIVED - International Partners and Organizations

## We have archived this page and will not be updating it.
You can use it for research or reference.

## We have archived this page and will not be updating it.
You can use it for research or reference.
To fulfill its mandate and meet its international commitments and objectives, Health Canada must work in collaboration with a number of actors, both in Canada and abroad. These partners are generally:
- Non- governmental organizations,
- Other federal departments and agencies,
- International and regional organizations,
- International financial institutions and other stakeholders.
- Pan American Health Organization Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) Initiative Proposed Harmonized Requirements for the Licensing of Vaccines in the Americas Guidelines for Preparation of Applications
- Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) Initiative Proposed Harmonized Requirements for the Licensing of Vaccines in the Americas Guidelines for Preparation of Applications
- Proposed Harmonized Requirements for the Licensing of Vaccines in the Americas
- Guidelines for Preparation of Applications
- World Health Organization
Multilateral relations are an important part of Canada's foreign policy activities. Health Canada is engaged with a range of international organizations in extensive work to help shape the international agenda on health and health care issues.
Global Health Research Initiative (GHRI) Operational Research Grants for the Canadian International Immunization Initiative (CIII2)
